scope_note: Prototype review for S033 (Files tab CRUD enhancements). 7 mocks covering create / rename / delete / move / multi-select. Approved through 4 rounds of refinement with the user.
design_decisions: + New file / + New folder are 2 compact icon buttons (📄+ / 📁+) at bottom-right of the file list, not large rows. Matches VS Code explorer header style. Tooltip carries label.
Inline create row appears at the END of the current dir's listing (above the CTAs). User feedback discarded the alternative of inserting at click position — cleaner spatial coherence.
Inline rename happens IN PLACE on the row that's being renamed. Basename pre-selected; extension preserved.
Right-click context menu rendered at viewport level (portal in production). Items: Open / Rename… / Move… / Copy path / Open on GitHub / Delete (danger, red).
Delete: dedicated confirm modal with per-item path + size + last-modified breakdown. Recursive directory delete is flagged. Single and batch share the same modal.

Move: dedicated modal with repo-root-relative path input + ⌘K-@-style incremental directory completion + per-item FROM→TO live preview. Single = full path, batch = target directory only (basenames preserved).

Multi-select uses Finder/VSCode-style tinted background + 2px accent left-border ONLY — no checkboxes. Earlier checkbox prototypes (slot-saving icon swap, etc.) were rejected because they forced a file/folder color hack that felt unnatural.
Touch multi-select: long-press to enter select mode (>=500ms). Once in mode, tap toggles instead of opens. Action bar Cancel exits the mode.

Action bar appears when ≥1 item is selected: 'N selected · → Move… · 🗑 Delete · ✕ Cancel'. Right-click on any selected row shows the BATCH variant of the context menu (Move N items… / Delete N items / Copy N paths) with Rename and Open hidden because those are single-item operations.

Browser <button> default ButtonFace background was leaking through the file rows during prototype iteration. Reset .fileRow with `background: transparent; border: 0; width: 100%;` — added to the prototype CSS to keep production-CSS aware of the gotcha.
